title: isoetes muricata subsp. maritima
intro: Isoetes muricata subsp. maritima, also known as sea quillwort, is a subspecies of aquatic plant that belongs to the quillwort family, characterized by its long, narrow leaves resembling quills. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin infections. The health benefits of Isoetes muricata subsp. maritima include its ability to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
